**Adreno (formerly ATI Mobile Graphics)** is a series of [graphics processing unit](/wiki/Graphics_processing_unit "Graphics processing unit") (GPU) [semiconductor intellectual property cores](/wiki/Semiconductor_intellectual_property_core "Semiconductor intellectual property core") developed by [Qualcomm](/wiki/Qualcomm "Qualcomm") and used in many of their [SoCs](/wiki/System_on_a_chip "System on a chip").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adreno&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Adreno is an integrated graphics processing unit (GPU) within Qualcomm's Snapdragon applications processors, that was jointly developed by [ATI Technologies](/wiki/ATI_Technologies "ATI Technologies") in conjunction with Qualcomm's preexisting "QShader" GPU architecture, and coalesced into a single family of GPUs that rebranded as Adreno in 2008, just prior to AMD's mobile division being sold to [Qualcomm](/wiki/Qualcomm "Qualcomm") in January 2009 for $65M.[[1]](#cite_note-pcper-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) Early Adreno models included the Adreno 100 and 110, which had 2D graphics acceleration and limited multimedia capabilities. Prior to 2008, 3D graphics on mobile platforms were commonly handled using software-based rendering engines, which limited their performance and consumed too much power to be used for anything other than rudimentary mobile graphics applications. With growing demand for more advanced multimedia and 3D graphics capabilities, Qualcomm licensed the [Imageon](/wiki/Imageon "Imageon") IP from AMD, in order to add hardware-accelerated 3D capabilities to their mobile products.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Further collaboration with AMD resulted in the development of the Adreno 200, originally named the AMD Z430, based on a mobile Imageon variant of the R400[[4]](#cite_note-4) architecture used in the [Xenos](/wiki/Xenos_(graphics_chip) "Xenos (graphics chip)") GPU of the [Xbox 360](/wiki/Xbox_360 "Xbox 360") video game console[[5]](#cite_note-5) and released in 2008, which was integrated into the first [Snapdragon](/wiki/Qualcomm_Snapdragon "Qualcomm Snapdragon") [SoC](/wiki/System_on_a_chip "System on a chip"). In January 2009, AMD sold their entire [Imageon](/wiki/Imageon "Imageon") handheld device graphics division to Qualcomm.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

### Adreno 700 series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adreno&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Adreno 700 series")]

* All models support the following [APIs](/wiki/Application_programming_interface "Application programming interface"): [Direct3D](/wiki/Direct3D "Direct3D") 12\_1, [OpenCL](/wiki/OpenCL "OpenCL") 3.0, [OpenGL ES](/wiki/OpenGL_ES "OpenGL ES") 3.2 and [Vulkan](/wiki/Vulkan_(API) "Vulkan (API)") 1.1
* **Adreno 740** is the first GPU from Qualcomm to feature [ray tracing](/wiki/Ray_tracing_(graphics) "Ray tracing (graphics)")
* Modern APIs and Display Support: The GPUs support modern graphics APIs like [OpenGL ES 3.2](/wiki/OpenGL_ES "OpenGL ES"), [OpenCL 2.0](/wiki/OpenCL_2.0 "OpenCL 2.0"), [DirectX 12](/wiki/DirectX "DirectX"), and [Vulkan](/wiki/Vulkan "Vulkan"). They are also capable of driving high-resolution and high-refresh-rate displays, such as WFHD+ at 168 Hz and [4K](/wiki/4K_resolution "4K resolution") at 60 Hz with support for [HDR10+](/wiki/HDR10%2B "HDR10+"), [HLG](/wiki/HLG10 "HLG10"), and [Dolby Vision](/wiki/Dolby_Vision "Dolby Vision").

### Adreno 800 series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adreno&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Adreno 800 series")]

* Uses a sliced architecture, with 3 slices with 4 CUs and 4 MB cache each in the Adreno 830.
* UBWC v6: The architecture incorporates version 6 of Universal Bandwidth Compression (UBWC).

* **Adreno 130** inside the MSM7x01, and MSM7x01A. It supports OpenGL ES 1.1, OpenVG 1.1, EGL 1.3, [Direct3D Mobile](/wiki/Direct3D_Mobile "Direct3D Mobile"), [SVGT](/wiki/Scalable_Vector_Graphics#SVG_Tiny_Support "Scalable Vector Graphics") 1.2, Direct Draw and [GDI](/wiki/Graphics_Device_Interface "Graphics Device Interface").
* **Adreno 200** (AMD Z430) inside the QSD8x50 and MSM7x27 (133 MHz). It offers a programmable function pipeline and streaming textures with support for OpenGL ES 1.0, OpenGL ES 1.1, OpenVG 1.1, EGL 1.4, Direct3D Mobile, SVGT 1.2 and DirectDraw. (22M triangles/second, 133M pixels/second, clock speed up to 133 MHz)
* **Adreno 200 enhanced** inside the MSM7x25A and MSM7x27A (200 MHz). It supports OpenGL ES 2.0, OpenGL ES 1.1, OpenVG 1.1, EGL 1.4, Direct3D Mobile, SVGT 1.2, Direct Draw and GDI. (40M triangles/second, 200M pixels/second, clock speed up to 200 MHz)
* **Adreno 203** inside the MSM8225 and MSM8225Q (400 MHz). It is an improvement over Adreno 205. It features a higher frequency, has better pixel fillrate, lower power consumption, better 3D performance. It is about 50-100% faster than Adreno 200 (enhanced), and 10–25% than Adreno 205. It could clock 2x times higher than Adreno 205. It supports OpenGL ES 2.0, OpenGL ES 1.1, OpenVG 1.1, EGL 1.4, Direct3D Mobile, DirectX 9.0c, SVGT 1.2, Direct Draw and GDI. (42-50M triangles/second, 250-300M pixels/second, clock speed from 192 to 400 MHz)
* **Adreno 205** inside the QSD8x50A, MSM7x30, and MSM8x55 (245 MHz). Its improvements include Hardware-accelerated SVG and [Adobe Flash](/wiki/Adobe_Flash "Adobe Flash") and better shader-performance than the Adreno 200. It supports OpenGL ES 2.0, OpenGL ES 1.1, OpenVG 1.1, EGL 1.4, Direct3D Mobile, SVGT 1.2, Direct Draw and GDI. (57M triangles/second, 250M pixels/second, clock speed up to 400 MHz)
* **Adreno 220** inside the MSM8660 or MSM8260 (266 MHz) with single channel memory. It supports OpenGL ES 2.0, OpenGL ES 1.1, OpenVG 1.1, EGL 1.4, Direct3D Mobile, DirectX 9.0c, SVGT 1.2, Direct Draw and GDI. (88M triangles/second, 500M pixels/second, standard clock speed up to 266 MHz, overclock up to 400 MHz)
* **Adreno 225** inside the MSM8960 (400 MHz), with unified shader architecture and dual channel memory. It supports Direct3D 9.0c in addition to OpenGL ES 2.0, OpenGL ES 1.1, OpenVG 1.1, EGL 1.4, Direct3D Mobile, SVGT 1.2, Direct Draw and GDI.
* **Adreno 320** inside the Qualcomm S4 Pro & Prime Series, with unified shader architecture and dual channel memory. It supports [Direct3D feature level](/wiki/Direct3D_feature_level "Direct3D feature level") 9\_3 in addition to OpenGL ES 3.0, OpenGL ES 2.0, OpenGL ES 1.1, OpenVG 1.1, EGL 1.4, Direct3D Mobile, SVGT 1.2[[22]](#cite_note-anandtech.com-23)
* **Adreno 330** inside the Nexus 5, Amazon Kindle HDX series tablets, Amazon Fire phone, Nokia Lumia 2520 tablet, Nokia Lumia 1520, Nokia Lumia Icon, Nokia Lumia 930, Samsung Galaxy S5, Samsung Galaxy Note 3, Sony Xperia Z1, Sony Xperia Z1 Compact, Sony Xperia Z2, Sony Xperia Z3, Sony Xperia Z3 Compact, Sony Xperia Z Ultra, Xiaomi Mi3, Xiaomi Mi4, OnePlus One, HTC One (M8) and LG G2/G3 smartphones.
* **Adreno 420** inside the Qualcomm Snapdragon 805 supports Direct3D 11.2 runtime ([feature level 11\_1](/wiki/Direct3D_feature_level "Direct3D feature level")). Inside the Google Nexus 6, Samsung Galaxy S5 LTE-A, Samsung Galaxy Note 4, Samsung Galaxy Note Edge, LG G3 Cat. 6, Amazon Fire HDX 8.9 (2014). The Qualcomm Snapdragon 805 is the first phone SoC ever to feature a 128-bit memory bus.
* **Adreno 540** inside the Qualcomm Snapdragon 835 is the first phone SoC to feature [variable refresh rate](/wiki/Variable_refresh_rate "Variable refresh rate") and [foveated rendering](/wiki/Foveated_rendering "Foveated rendering")/Variate Rate Shading, Qualcomm calls their implementations Q-Sync and Adreno Foveation.
* **Adreno 630** inside the Qualcomm Snapdragon 845 is the first phone SoC to feature Inside-Out [Room-scale](/wiki/Room_scale "Room scale") [6DoF](/wiki/Six_degrees_of_freedom "Six degrees of freedom") with [SLAM](/wiki/Simultaneous_localization_and_mapping "Simultaneous localization and mapping").
* **Adreno 640** inside the Qualcomm Snapdragon 855 is the first phone SoC to feature updateable GPU drivers from the [Google Play Store](/wiki/Google_Play_Store "Google Play Store").

### Operating system support

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adreno&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Operating system support")]

See also: [Free and open-source graphics device driver § Qualcomm](/wiki/Free_and_open-source_graphics_device_driver#Qualcomm "Free and open-source graphics device driver")

There are proprietary drivers for the [Linux](/wiki/Linux "Linux")-based mobile [operating system](/wiki/Operating_system "Operating system") [Android](/wiki/Android_(operating_system) "Android (operating system)") available from Qualcomm themselves.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
Historically the only way to have GPU support on non-Android Linux was with the [libhybris](/wiki/Hybris_(software) "Hybris (software)") wrapper.

Linux and [Mesa](/wiki/Mesa_(computer_graphics) "Mesa (computer graphics)") supports the Adreno 200/300/400/500 series of GPUs with a driver called *`freedreno`.* Freedreno allows fully open-source graphics on devices like the [96Boards Dragonboard 410c](/wiki/96Boards "96Boards") and [Nexus 7 (2013)](/wiki/Nexus_7_(2013) "Nexus 7 (2013)").

Qualcomm also provides Adreno drivers for ARM64 versions of Microsoft Windows.

Since [Linux kernel](/wiki/Linux_kernel "Linux kernel") 6.11, the mainline Linux kernel has added Adreno drivers for Qualcomm Snapdragon X system-on-a-chips.[[114]](#cite_note-115)
